senses.definition: a substance, such as glue, paste, or tape, that is used to bind two or more surfaces together by creating a bond that resists separation

a substance or material, often in liquid or paste form, used for filling gaps, cracks, or joints to create an airtight or watertight seal, typically in construction, plumbing, or automotive applications

examples.x: The plumber used sealant around the edges of the sink to prevent water from leaking.
To keep the windows airtight, we applied a silicone sealant along the edges.
A waterproof sealant was applied to the roof to prevent rain from seeping through.
The mechanic used sealant to fix the small crack in the car’s radiator.
The contractor recommended using a strong sealant to protect the outdoor furniture from the elements.

senses.definition: a thin, non-adhesive tape used to create a tight and leak-free seal on threaded pipe connections

examples.x: The plumber used thread seal tape to ensure a secure connection between the pipes.
If you notice a small leak around the pipe joints, try adding more thread seal tape.
Always apply thread seal tape when installing a new faucet to avoid water leakage.
Before tightening the pipe, make sure to wrap the thread seal tape around the male threads to prevent any leaks.
Thread seal tape is essential when working with gas lines to ensure safety and prevent leaks.

senses.definition: a flexible tool used by electricians to route and pull electrical wires or cables through conduit, walls, or other confined spaces

examples.x: The electrician used fish tape to pull the wires through the pipes without damaging them.
To install the new cables, the plumber carefully fed the fish tape through the narrow pipe.
Before starting the job, make sure you have a reel of fish tape on hand to help with the wiring.
Using fish tape, the workers managed to install the electrical cables in the tight space under the floor.

senses.definition: a type of insulating tape made of a thin, flexible material, typically vinyl or rubber, and is used to insulate and protect electrical connections and wires

examples.x: He used electrical tape to cover the exposed wires and prevent any electrical shock.
Before installing the new light fixture, make sure to wrap the connections with electrical tape.
The repairman used electrical tape to seal the frayed wire and ensure it worked safely.
I need some electrical tape to cover these wires in the circuit box.

senses.definition: a silver-colored object that is sticky on one side and is used for fixing things

examples.x: He used duct tape to temporarily patch the hole in the inflatable boat.
The handyman used duct tape to secure the loose wires behind the television.
She wrapped duct tape around the broken handle of her suitcase to keep it together.
The mechanic used duct tape to fix a tear in the car's upholstery.
During camping, duct tape proved invaluable for repairing torn tents and broken equipment.

senses.definition: a wide range of tapes that have an adhesive coating on one side, which allows them to stick to various surfaces for bonding, sealing, or masking purposes

examples.x: I used adhesive tape to seal the box before mailing it.
The repairman applied adhesive tape to fix the loose wiring in the wall.
She wrapped the gift with colorful adhesive tape for a fun touch.
Adhesive tape works well for temporary fixes until you can find a permanent solution.
I couldn’t find the stapler, so I used adhesive tape to hold the papers together.

senses.definition: a type of tape used in drywall installation and repair to reinforce and conceal joints between drywall panels

examples.x: I ran out of drywall tape and had to make a quick trip to the hardware store.
The drywall tape is essential for preventing cracks in the wall joints after the compound dries.
She used fiberglass mesh drywall tape because it was self-adhesive and easier to apply.
When installing new drywall, it is important to use drywall tape to keep the seams strong and even.
After applying the drywall tape, he smoothed out the compound to fill the gaps between the panels.

senses.definition: a type of adhesive tape that is commonly used in painting and other projects to temporarily protect surfaces and create clean, straight lines

examples.x: She used masking tape to protect the baseboards before painting the walls.
The artist carefully applied masking tape to create sharp edges on the canvas.
Make sure to remove the masking tape right after the paint dries to avoid peeling.
He covered the electrical outlets with masking tape to keep them free of paint.
The DIY enthusiast used masking tape to mark the areas that needed sanding.

senses.definition: a type of adhesive tape that contains reflective material, designed to enhance visibility and improve safety in low-light or dark conditions

examples.x: The emergency exit signs were outlined with reflective tape so they would be visible in case of a power outage.
I added reflective tape to the back of my bicycle to stay safe when riding in the dark.
The road signs had reflective tape to ensure drivers could see them even in low-light conditions.
The safety vest had bright reflective tape on it to help workers be seen in dimly lit areas.
I wrapped reflective tape around the edges of the stairs to prevent accidents in the evening.

senses.definition: a brightly colored, non-adhesive tape used to mark off restricted or hazardous areas and to create a visible barrier for safety purposes

examples.x: The police put up barrier tape around the accident scene to keep the area secure.
Barrier tape was stretched across the damaged road to warn drivers of the hazard ahead.
We placed barrier tape around the wet floor to prevent people from slipping.
After the flood, barrier tape was used to block off the affected areas to prevent entry.

senses.definition: a portable, often metal box for organizing and keeping tools in

examples.x: He kept his wrenches, screwdrivers, and pliers neatly organized in his toolbox.
The handyman carried his toolbox to the job site to have all the necessary tools at hand.
She rummaged through the toolbox to find the right size socket wrench for the project.
The mechanic's toolbox contained specialized tools for automotive repairs.
The carpenter's toolbox was a well-worn wooden chest filled with saws, hammers, and measuring tapes.

senses.definition: a waist-worn belt or apron used by professionals to carry and access tools while working
clothing specifically designed to be worn while performing manual labor or other physical work
protective eyewear used to prevent eye injuries in hazardous environments, commonly worn in industrial, construction, and laboratory settings
a device worn over the ears to reduce or block out noise, often used as hearing protection in loud or noisy environments
a protective glove used to safeguard the hands against workplace hazards, commonly used in industrial, construction, and other settings
a light rigid headgear worn by workers, etc. to protect their heads
a type of personal protective equipment consisting of straps and connectors worn by workers to prevent falls and protect them from injury when working at heights
a structure or device designed to prevent or reduce the risk of accidents, danger, or damage in a particular area or situation

examples.x: The construction site was surrounded by a safety barrier to keep people out of the dangerous area.
Workers set up a safety barrier around the hole in the ground to prevent accidents.
The highway had a safety barrier in the middle to separate traffic traveling in opposite directions.
The company installed safety barriers at the factory to protect employees from moving machinery.
The safety barrier along the cliff edge ensured that hikers stayed a safe distance from the drop.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment with a set of steps that are connected to two long bars, used for climbing up and down a height

examples.x: The ladder was too short to reach the attic, so they had to find a longer one.
She carefully placed the ladder against the roof before starting her repairs.
The firefighter quickly climbed the ladder to rescue the cat stuck in the tree.
The painter set up his ladder against the wall to reach the highest part of the house.
He used a ladder to reach the top shelf in the garage and grab the toolbox.

senses.definition: a small, portable ladder with steps, used for reaching higher places safely

examples.x: He used the step ladder to reach the top shelf in the kitchen.
I keep a step ladder in the garage for cleaning the windows.
She climbed the step ladder carefully to change the lightbulb.
The step ladder folds up easily, so it fits in the closet when not in use.
The step ladder helped me get to the back of the cabinet without trouble.

senses.definition: a device or instrument designed to be operated by hand, typically without the use of power, and used for various tasks such as cutting, gripping, fastening, measuring, or manipulating materials

examples.x: He grabbed a hand tool from his toolbox to fix the leaky faucet.
The carpenter used a hand tool to carefully carve the wooden frame.
I need a hand tool to tighten this screw before the table falls apart.
The mechanic reached for a hand tool to remove the bolts on the engine.
She prefers using hand tools because they give her more control over the work.

senses.definition: a device used to securely connect or join objects together, providing stability and strength through mechanical means, such as screws, bolts, or nails

a mechanical device or instrument that is powered by electricity, battery, or compressed air, designed to perform various tasks with increased efficiency and reduced manual effort, such as cutting, drilling, sanding, or shaping materials

examples.x: He used a power tool to drill holes in the wall for the new shelves.
The carpenter relied on a power tool to cut through the thick wood quickly.
She bought a power tool set that included a drill, sander, and saw.
He wore safety goggles while using the power tool to grind the metal surface.
The construction workers carried a variety of power tools to the site to complete the project.

senses.definition: a tool with bristles made of wire, used for cleaning, rust removal, and surface preparation in various applications

examples.x: He used a wire brush to remove the rust from the metal surface before painting it.
The mechanic grabbed a wire brush to clean the engine parts and ensure they were free of dirt.
She scrubbed the old grill with a wire brush to get rid of the grease and food residue.
A wire brush is the best tool for cleaning off tough stains on metal or concrete.
To restore the antique tool, they carefully scrubbed it with a wire brush to remove years of built-up grime.

senses.definition: a protective sheet, usually made of fabric or plastic, that is placed on the floor or furniture to prevent paint, debris, or other substances from damaging or staining the surface during various projects such as painting, construction, or home improvement
a tool used in masonry and tile work, typically made of fabric or plastic, that is filled with grout

examples.x: The worker filled the grout bag with the mixture to seal the tile joints.
After mixing the grout, she carefully squeezed the grout bag to fill the gaps between the tiles.
He used a grout bag to ensure the grout was applied evenly along the edges of the floor tiles.
A grout bag is essential for precise grout application in small or hard-to-reach areas.
They used a grout bag to apply the final layer of grout before cleaning the tile surface.

senses.definition: a type of colored liquid or pigment that is applied to wood surfaces to enhance their appearance, add color, and provide protection

examples.x: He applied a dark wood stain to the table to give it a more elegant appearance.
The old bookshelf looked new again after we used wood stain to brighten its surface.
She chose a light wood stain to match the rest of the furniture in the room.
The carpenter recommended using a wood stain to bring out the natural grain of the oak.
He used a walnut wood stain on the chair to achieve a rich, warm color.

senses.definition: a shallow, flat container with reservoirs or compartments used for holding and distributing paint during painting projects, typically used with a paint roller or brush for easy access and application of paint

examples.x: He poured the paint into the paint tray before starting to work on the walls.
After dipping the roller in the paint tray, she carefully applied it to the surface.
I need to clean the paint tray before using it for the next coat.
She used a paint tray to hold the primer while she painted the baseboards.

senses.definition: a shallow, flat container typically made of plastic or metal, used for mixing various substances or materials, such as paints, adhesives, or construction compounds

examples.x: The workers filled the mixing tray with plaster to repair the damaged wall.
You can use a mixing tray to blend the grout before applying it to the tiles.
She poured the cement into the mixing tray to ensure an even consistency for the project.
After adding the ingredients, he stirred them together in the mixing tray to create a smooth paste.
The technician grabbed a mixing tray to mix the epoxy resin for the repair job.
